message:
fix(core): prevent spurious SQLITE_SCHEMA errors during batched VTAB operations

During long-running batched INSERT...SELECT into FTS5 virtual tables,
memdb reloads could see a schema_cookie mismatch on page1 and blindly
increment schema_generation, invalidating all prepared statements even
though the actual schema structure was unchanged.

Three coordinated fixes:
- Capture structural fingerprints (table names, root pages, column
  counts, index counts) before/after memdb reload; only bump
  schema_generation when the structure actually differs
- After file-backed autocommit, sync the connection's commit clock
  floor with the pager's published snapshot to prevent spurious
  memdb reloads on the next operation
- Track pending_local_live_vtab_preserve_once to let same-connection
  reads reuse live VTAB instances after a self-commit

Closes #65

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
